chunks: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) is the National Standards Body of India working under the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution, Government of India. It is established by the Bureau of Indian Standards Act 2016. Core functions include Standards Formulation, Product Certification, Hallmarking of Gold and Silver Jewellery, L

aboratory Services, Training Services and Consumer Affairs. BIS promotes standardization, marking and quality certification of goods. BIS has regional offices across India and operates through its head office in New Delhi.

BIS Product Certification scheme certifies that a product conforms to a specific Indian Standard. The ISI Mark is the most popular certification mark in India. Products can be certified voluntarily or mandatorily. Mandatory certification covers products affecting health and safety such as electrical appliances, cement, steel, LPG cylinders, helmets
, and food products. The ISI mark guarantees quality, safety and reliability of products. Manufacturers must apply to BIS and get their products tested in BIS recognized laboratories.

Steps to apply for BIS certification: 1) Visit the BIS portal manakonline.in and register as a manufacturer. 2) Select the applicable Indian Standard for your product. 3) Fill the application form and upload required documents including factory details, equipment list, and quality control details. 4) Pay the application fee and marking fee. 5) A BI

S officer will visit your factory for inspection. 6) Send product samples to a BIS recognized laboratory for testing. 7) If all tests pass, BIS will issue the license allowing use of ISI mark on your product. The entire process takes approximately 30-90 days depending on product category.

Documents required for BIS certification application include: Manufacturer registration certificate, Factory license, Product details and specifications, Quality control manual, List of testing equipment, Layout plan of factory, Details of key personnel, Previous test reports if any, Trademark registration (if applicable), and Bank details. All doc

BIS Hallmarking is a quality certification of precious metal articles in India. Hallmarking of gold jewellery is mandatory in India since 2023. The BIS hallmark consists of: 1) BIS logo, 2) Purity grade such as 916 for 22 karat gold, 585 for 14 karat gold, 375 for 9 karat gold, 999 for 24 karat gold, 925 for silver, 3) A unique HUID (Hallmark Uniqu
e Identification) number. Consumers can verify hallmark genuineness using the BIS Care mobile app. Jewellers need a BIS licence to sell hallmarked jewellery.

To apply for BIS hallmarking licence: 1) The jewellery must be manufactured by a BIS licensed jeweller. 2) Submit application through BIS portal. 3) The jewellery is tested and hallmarking is done at a BIS recognized hallmarking centre. 4) Each piece gets a unique HUID number. 5) The hallmark includes BIS logo, purity mark, and assaying and hallmar
king centre mark. Hallmarking ensures consumers get the correct purity of gold and silver jewellery they pay for.

BIS formulates Indian Standards through a consultative process involving industry, consumers, scientific institutions and government. There are more than 20000 Indian Standards in force covering various sectors including food, agriculture, chemicals, civil engineering, electrical, electronics, mechanical, metallurgical, medical equipment and textil
es. Standards can be purchased from the BIS website at bis.gov.in or from standardsbis.in portal.

BIS has a network of NABL accredited laboratories to support certification and testing activities. Services include testing of products for certification, calibration services, and training. BIS maintains a list of recognized laboratories across India for product testing. Lab fees and list of recognized labs are available at crsbis.in. Manufacturer
s can get their products tested at any BIS recognized laboratory for certification purposes.

BIS runs consumer awareness programmes to educate people about standardization and quality. Consumers can report fake ISI mark products via BIS Care mobile app, the bis.gov.in portal, or any BIS regional or branch office. BIS conducts raids and takes legal action against manufacturers misusing the ISI mark. BIS organizes consumer connect programmes
across India. Consumers can also check authenticity of BIS certified products online.

To file a complaint with BIS about fake or substandard products: 1) Download BIS Care app from Play Store/App Store. 2) Register your complaint with product details, purchase receipt, and photos. 3) BIS will investigate and take action against the violator. 4) You can also complain via the BIS website or visit nearest BIS office. Complaints are typ

BIS conformity assessment schemes include: 1) Product Certification Scheme with ISI Mark for industrial products. 2) Compulsory Registration Scheme (CRS) for electronics and IT products like mobile phones, laptops, LED lights and power banks. 3) Foreign Manufacturers Certification Scheme (FMCS) for imported products. 4) Management System Certificat
ion for ISO standards. 5) Hallmarking Scheme for gold and silver jewellery. 6) Eco Mark Scheme for environment friendly products.

The Compulsory Registration Scheme (CRS) under BIS makes it mandatory for electronics and IT products to be registered before sale in India. Products covered include: mobile phones, laptops, tablets, LED lights, power banks, smart watches, printers,fax machines, microwave ovens, and electric irons. Foreign manufacturers must appoint an Authorized I
ndian Representative (AIR) to register their products. Registration is valid for 2 years and can be renewed.

The Foreign Manufacturers Certification Scheme (FMCS) allows foreign manufacturers to obtain BIS certification for products exported to India. The foreign manufacturer applies through an Authorized Indian Representative (AIR) based in India. BIS conducts factory inspection abroad either directly or through third party. Products must conform to rele
vant Indian Standards. FMCS ensures imported products meet the same quality standards as domestically manufactured products.

BIS represents India in international and regional standards bodies including ISO (International Organization for Standardization) and IEC (International Electrotechnical Commission). BIS has signed MoUs with national standards bodies of several countries for mutual cooperation. BIS participates in Pacific Area Standards Congress (PASC) and South A
sian Regional Standards Organization (SARSO). International cooperation helps align Indian Standards with global standards and facilitates trade.

BIS is established under the Bureau of Indian Standards Act 2016 which replaced the BIS Act 1986. The Act empowers the government to make certification of any article compulsory. The BIS Conformity Assessment Regulations 2018 govern the certification process. Misuse of BIS certification marks is a punishable offence under the Act with penalties inc
luding imprisonment and fine. BIS can conduct raids and take legal action against violators.

BIS certification fees include: non-refundable application fee (varies by product category), Annual Minimum Marking Fee (AMMF), and marking fee based on production quantity. For ISI certification, fees range from Rs. 5,000 to Rs. 50,000 depending on product type. Testing charges are separate and paid to the testing laboratory. For exact fee details
visit manakonline.in or contact nearest BIS regional or branch office.

Consumers can file complaints against fake BIS certified products via: BIS Care mobile app, consumer portal on bis.gov.in, or any BIS regional or branch office. BIS has regional offices in major cities including Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai, Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Ahmedabad. Enforcement actions include raids, seizures and legal proceedings ag
ainst violators. For grievances related to BIS services, complaints can be filed online at bis.gov.in.

BIS publishes annual reports, Indian Standards, technical guides and consumer awareness materials. Annual reports are available on the BIS website. Standards can be purchased online from bis.gov.in or from the Standards Sale Counter at BIS head office. BIS also publishes handbooks and special publications for various sectors. Press releases and new
s updates are regularly published on the BIS website.

The BIS online portal manakonline.in is the official platform for all certification related activities. Manufacturers can: register online, submit application for product certification, track application status, pay fees online, download license certificates, and manage their profile. The portal also provides list of Indian Standards, recognized la

The ISI Mark is a certification mark issued by BIS for product quality and safety. Products bearing ISI mark have been tested as conforming to Indian Standards. Benefits of ISI mark include: consumer trust and confidence, legal acceptance as proof of quality, competitive advantage in market, and compliance with regulatory requirements. The ISI mark
is one of the most recognized quality marks in India.

BIS has regional offices in major Indian cities: Delhi (North), Mumbai (West), Kolkata (East), Chennai (South), Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Ahmedabad. Additionally, BIS has branch offices in over 30 cities across India. Contact details are available on bis.gov.in. Customers can visit nearest office for certification queries, complaints, and general i

FOSS × BIS Hackathon  —  Problem Statement & Test Cases    |    CONFIDENTIAL — DO NOT DISTRIBUTE 
BIS × FOSS Hackathon 2026  —  AI Website Chatbot ChallengePage 1 
 
🏆  FOSS × BIS HACKATHON 
AI-Powered Website Chatbot Challenge 
Problem Statement, Test Cases & Judging Criteria  —  24-Hour Sprint 
 
1. Challenge Overview 
You have 24 hours to build

an AI-powered chatbot that can answer any question about the BIS website 
(bis.gov.in) — accurately, conversationally, and with source citations. The core technology is a RAG (Retrieval-
Augmented Generation) pipeline that you will build from scratch. 
 
The chatbot must crawl, index, and understand content from across the full BIS website — includ

ing standards, 
certification schemes, laboratory services, consumer programmes, publications, press releases, and more — and 
surface precise answers from anywhere on the site. 
 
The BIS Website — Your Knowledge Source 
The Bureau of Indian Standards (bis.gov.in) is India's national standards body. Its website spans 
dozens of pages covering prod

uct certification schemes, laboratory accreditation, standards 
development, consumer affairs, international relations, publications, legislation, and more. Your 
chatbot must index and understand all of it. 
 
 
2. Problem Statement 
The BIS website currently has no intelligent assistant. Users searching for certification processes, scheme details

, 
fee structures, or policy information must manually navigate through multiple pages, skim through content, and 
piece together answers themselves. This creates three distinct problems: 
 
    
Problem 1 — Search Returns Links, Not Answers 
Traditional keyword search returns a list of pages. Users must read, skim, and guess which section 
contain

s what they need. 
       
Problem 2 — Content is Scattered Across Many Pages 
The BIS website spans dozens of pages across standards, schemes, labs, consumer programmes, 
publications, and more. Answers are often split across multiple sections.

Problem 3 — Frustration Drives Disengagement 
When users cannot find answers, they raise support queries or abandon the site entirely. Poor 
discoverability is a silent driver of disengagement. 
 
 
3. What You Are Building \n\nFOSS × BIS Hackathon  —  Problem Statement & Test Cases    |    CONFIDENTIAL — DO NOT DISTRIBUTE 
BIS × FOSS Hackathon 202

6  —  AI Website Chatbot ChallengePage 2 
A drop-in chatbot widget powered by a custom RAG pipeline that ingests and indexes the full BIS website and 
answers user questions with grounded, cited responses. 
 
Required Pipeline 
 
Stage Name What Happens 
1 Ingest Recursively crawl all pages across bis.gov.in — standards, certification, labs, 
publi

cations, news, FAQs, consumer programmes 
2 Chunk Split content into ~500-token overlapping passages; preserve headings and 
structural units 
3 Embed Convert chunks to vector representations using an embedding model (e.g. text-
embedding-3-small) 
4 Store Index vectors + metadata (URL, title, content type, timestamp) in a vector 
database 
5 Retri

eve Find the top-K most relevant chunks for any user query via cosine similarity 
search 
6 Answer Pass retrieved context to an LLM with a grounding prompt; stream the response 
to the UI 
 
Required Deliverables 
• GitHub repository with all code written during the hackathon 
• README.md with setup instructions, architecture overview, and tools us

ed 
• Live deployed demo (Vercel, Railway, Render, or equivalent) 
• Architecture diagram showing the full RAG pipeline 
• Working chatbot indexed on real BIS website content 
 
 
4. Open Test Cases — Evaluated Live During Demo 
These 5 test cases are known to you in advance. Your chatbot will be asked these exact questions (or close 
variants) dur

ing the live demo. Each is worth 4 points. Judges will verify answer accuracy, source citation quality, 
and response behaviour. 
 
How Judging Works 
During your demo, judges will type each query directly into your chatbot. They wil

l assess: (a) 
whether the answer is factually correct and grounded in BIS website content, (b) whether a valid 
source URL is cited, and (c) whether the chatbot behaves gracefully under edge conditions. 
 
# Scenario What the Chatbot Must Do Pass Condition 
TC-01 Basic Fact RetrievalQuery: 
"What is BIS and what are 
its core functions?" 
Retrieve

relevant content from the About / 
Overview section of the BIS website and 
return an accurate, concise answer 
grounded in that content. 
Answer is factually correct, 
cites source URL, contains 
no fabricated claims. \n\nFOSS × BIS Hackathon  —  Problem Statement & Test Cases    |    CONFIDENTIAL — DO NOT DISTRIBUTE 
BIS × FOSS Hackathon 2026  —

AI Website Chatbot ChallengePage 3 
# Scenario What the Chatbot Must Do Pass Condition 
TC-02 Standards & 
CertificationQuery: "How do I 
apply for a BIS certification 
for my product?" 
Locate the certification / scheme pages, 
extract the correct process steps, and 
present them in a readable, structured 
format with source links. 
Correct proc

ess outlined, 
at least one source citation, 
no hallucinated 
requirements. 
TC-03 Multi-turn ConversationTurn 
1: "What schemes does BIS 
offer?"Turn 2: "Tell me more 
about the third one." 
Maintain conversation context across turns. 
Turn 2 must resolve 'the third one' without 
the user restating the topic. 
Turn 2 correctly references 
the sch

eme named in Turn 
1. No context loss. 
TC-04 Cross-Section 
RetrievalQuery: "What is BIS 
doing in the area of 
consumer awareness?" 
Pull relevant content from multiple sections 
(e.g. press releases, programmes, 
publications) and synthesise a coherent 
answer. 
Answer draws from 2+ 
content types, cites distinct 
source URLs, answer is 
coheren

t. 
TC-05 Graceful Out-of-Scope 
HandlingQuery: "What is the 
current stock price of Tata 
Steel?" 
Recognise the query is outside the scope of 
the indexed BIS website and respond 
gracefully without fabricating an answer. 
Chatbot declines to 
answ

er, explains it can 
only answer from BIS 
website content, suggests 
a relevant BIS topic if 
possible. 
 
 
5. Hidden Test Cases — Revealed After Submission 
These 10 additional scenarios will be evaluated after submissions close. You will not see the exact queries in 
advance. They are designed to stress-test the depth of your RAG pipeline acros

s harder retrieval scenarios, multi-
turn memory, and adversarial probes. 
 
🔒 Why Hidden Test Cases? 
The hidden cases reward teams who build genuinely robust systems rather than demo-polished 
prototypes. They test cross-page synthesis, hallucination resistance, precise fact retrieval, multi-turn 
memory, and handling of ambiguous or adversarial

queries. Each hidden case is worth 2 points (20 
points total). 
 
# Domain Question Type Why It’s Hard 
HTC-
01 
Laboratory 
Services 
Specific service lookup Requires navigating nested lab/testing 
pages 
HTC-
02 
Legislation & Acts Policy/legal content Legal language needs precise retrieval, no 
paraphrase errors 
HTC-
03 
International 
Relatio

ns 
Cross-page synthesis Content spread across multiple 
international cooperation pages 
HTC-
04 
Grievance / 
Complaints 
Process + contact info Tests whether contact details and steps 
are retrieved together 
HTC-
05 
Publications & 
Reports 
Document discovery Must surface specific reports, not just the 
publications index 
HTC-
06 
Hallucinati

on probe Invented regulation check Query references a non-existent BIS rule 
— bot must not fabricate 
HTC-
07 
Multilingual intent English query, Hindi-labelled content Tests robustness when indexed content 
has mixed-language labels \n\nFOSS × BIS Hackathon  —  Problem Statement & Test Cases    |    CONFIDENTIAL — DO NOT DISTRIBUTE 
BIS × FOSS Ha

ckathon 2026  —  AI Website Chatbot ChallengePage 4 
# Domain Question Type Why It’s Hard 
HTC-
08 
Numerical/date 
facts 
Fee or timeline lookup Precise numeric facts must match source 
exactly 
HTC-
09 
Follow-up pronoun 
resolution 
Multi-turn (3

turns) Third turn uses ambiguous pronoun — 
context must be retained 
HTC-
10 
Comparative query Side-by-side scheme comparison Requires retrieving and comparing content 
from two separate pages 
 
Note: Exact queries for each hidden test case will be released to all teams simultaneously immediately after the 
submission deadline. Results will be e

valuated by the judging panel. 
 
 
6. Scoring Breakdown 
 
Category Points Notes 
Technical Implementation 25 End-to-end RAG pipeline, ingestion quality, retrieval 
accuracy 
Answer Quality & Accuracy 25 Grounded answers, zero hallucinations, correct 
citations 
5 Open Test Cases 20 4 points each — judged live during demo 
10 Hidden Test Cases 20

2 points each — evaluated post-submission 
Innovation & UX 10 Creative features, UI polish, unique approach 
TOTAL 100  
 
Tip: The open and hidden test cases together account for 40 points — nearly half the total score. Teams 
who invest in retrieval quality, grounding, and citation accuracy will have a significant advantage. 
 
 
7. What Judges L

ook For 
 
7.1 Technical Implementation (25 pts) 
• Does the ingestion pipeline handle the full breadth of content types on the BIS website? 
• Does semantic retrieval surface genuinely relevant chunks — not just keyword matches? 
• Is the LLM answer strictly grounded in retrieved context, with no fabrication? 
• Is the pipeline architecturally sou

nd and maintainable? 
 
7.2 Answer Quality & Accuracy (25 pts) 
• Every claim in the answer must be traceable to a retrieved chunk from the BIS website. 
• Zero hallucinations — if the answer is not in the indexed content, the bot must say so. 
• Source citations must link to the actual page where the content was found. \n\nFOSS × BIS Hackathon  —

Problem Statement & Test Cases    |    CONFIDENTIAL — DO NOT DISTRIBUTE 
BIS × FOSS Hackathon 2026  —  AI Website Chatbot ChallengePage 5 
• Multi-turn context must be maintained coherently across the conversation. 
 
7.3 Test Case Performance (40 p

ts) 
• 5 Open Cases (4 pts each): Evaluated live during the demo presentation. 
• 10 Hidden Cases (2 pts each): Evaluated by the panel after submission closes. 
• Partial credit may be awarded for partially correct or well-reasoned answers. 
 
7.4 Innovation & UX (10 pts) 
• Novel retrieval strategies: hybrid search, re-ranking, query rewriting. 
•

UI polish: streaming responses, readable citations, content-type labels. 
• Creative features: follow-up suggestions, scope filters, feedback mechanism. 
 
 
8. 24-Hour Timeline 
 
Time Window Phase Goal 
Hour 0–3 Setup & Scaffolding Project init, API keys, vector DB index, site crawl plan 
Hour 3–10 Ingestion Pipeline Crawler, chunking, embedding

, vector upsert — full BIS site 
indexed 
Hour 10–16 Query API Semantic search endpoint, prompt engineering, streaming 
response 
Hour 16–20 Chat Widget Frontend component, streaming display, source citations 
Hour 20–22 Polish & Testing Edge cases, cross-section retrieval quality, mobile 
responsiveness 
Hour 22–23 Demo Prep Final deploy, slides,

rehearsal against the 5 open test cases 
Hour 24 Submission + 
Presentations 
GitHub repo submitted; live demo to judges 
 
The 5 open test cases are published at Hour 0. Use them to guide your development and prioritise 
retrieval quality from the start. 
 
 
9. Rules Summary 
• Teams of 2–5 registered participants only. 
• All code must be writte

n during the hackathon window (Hour 0 to Hour 24). 
• The chatbot must be indexed on real BIS website content (bis.gov.in). 
• A working live demo must be presented — pre-recorded video alone is not accepted. 
• Any public LLM provider, vector database, and open-source library is permitted. 
• Starter code or boilerplate created before the event is

not permitted. 
• Source code must be submitted via the designated GitHub repository before the deadline. 
 \n\nFOSS × BIS Hackathon  —  Problem Statement & Test Cases    |    CONFIDENTIAL — DO NOT DISTRIBUTE 
BIS × FOSS Hackathon 2026  —  AI Websit

e Chatbot ChallengePage 6 
 
10. Suggested Tech Stack(Optional) 
The following is a recommendation, not a requirement. Use whatever tools let your team build the best system. 
 
Layer Recommended Options 
LLM Gemini 2.5,Llama 3,Mistral 7B,Mixtral 8x7B,Phi-3,Gemma 
Embeddings text-embedding-3-small, all-MiniLM-L6-v2 
Vector Database Pinecone, Weavia

te, Qdrant, pgvector 
Site Crawler Crawlee, Scrapy, Firecrawl, sitemap parsers 
RAG Framework LangChain.js, LlamaIndex, Haystack 
Backend Framework Next.js 15, Remix, FastAPI 
Streaming / Memory Redis, Upstash, Server-Sent Events (SSE) 
UI Components Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui 
Deployment Vercel, Railway, Render 
 
 
 
Good luck. Build something great

. 
FOSS × BIS Hackathon  —  AI Website Chatbot Challenge  —  24 Hours 
 \n\n       Draft Standards Preparation Activity  
Pre-Standardisation Study
Topic : Performance and Accuracy of Fitness Wearables
Submission link : https://forms.gle/Us5zR6FFGeNevcSs6  
Submission deadline : 12 March 2026
Activity     Overview      
Participants will take part

in a Draft Standards Preparation Activity, simulating the standard 
development process followed by the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS).
Each team will prepare a Pre-Standardisation Study Report proposing draft standards for the given 
topic.
The objective of this activity is to help participants understand:
 How product quality standards are dev

eloped
 How performance benchmarks are defined
 How testing methodologies are proposed
Topic     for     the     Study      
Performance and Accuracy of Fitness Wearables
Participants will study consumer wearable devices. These include:
• Smartwatches (e.g. Apple Watch, Samsung Galaxy Watch): multifunctional devices that track 
health metrics alo

ngside notifications and apps
• Fitness Bands (e.g. Mi Band, Fitbit): simpler, dedicated devices focused on activity and health 
tracking
• Health Monitoring Wearables (e.g. pulse oximeters, ECG patches): devices designed specifically 
to monitor med

ical or clinical health parameters
The goal is to propose standard guidelines for measuring how well these devices actually perform.  
In other words: when your watch says your heart rate is 72 BPM or you've walked 8,000 steps, how do we 
know if that's true?
Your report should define:
•  What should be tested (e.g. heart rate accuracy, step counti

ng)
• How it should be tested (e.g. comparing against medical-grade equipment)
• What counts as acceptable (e.g. heart rate readings within ±5 BPM of the actual value)\n\nThink of it as writing the rulebook that manufacturers would have to follow before claiming their device is
accurate.
What     Participants     Need     To     Do      
Participan

ts must prepare a technical report that includes:
1. Understanding the product category.
2. Identifying key performance parameters.
3. Studying existing practices or issues in the market.
4. Proposing testing methods.
5. Suggesting performance benchmarks for standardization.
Suggested     Report     Structure      
Participants are advised to follo

w the structure below.
1. Title Page
Example:
Pre-Standardisation Study on Performance and Accuracy of Fitness Wearables
Prepared by:
Team Name / Participants
Event:
Draft Standards Preparation Activity
Organized by:
FOSS NIT Srinagar in collaboration with BIS
2. Introduction
Explain:
 What fitness wearables are
 Their role in health and fitness

monitoring
 Why standardization is important
3. Scope of the Study
Define which devices are included. 
Example:
 Smartwatches\n\n Fitness bands
 Health monitoring wearables
Parameters considered:
 Heart rate monitoring
 Step counting
 Sleep tracking
 GPS tracking
 Battery performance
 4. Existing Practices
Participants may briefly discuss:

 Current market practices
 Accuracy claims by manufacturers
 Problems in measurement reliability
5.Key Performance Parameters
Examples include:
 Heart Rate Accuracy
 Step Counting Accuracy
 Sleep Tracking Reliability
 GPS Accuracy
 Battery L

ife
6.Proposed Testing Methodology
Participants should describe how each parameter should be tested. 
Example:
Heart Rate Testing
Comparison with medical grade ECG devices
Step Count Testing
Compare actual steps vs device recorded steps
Sleep Tracking
Compare wearable data with sleep monitoring systems
7.Proposed Performance Benchmarks 
Participant

s should define acceptable limits.\n\nExample:
Heart Rate Accuracy
±5 BPM
Step Count Accuracy
±3% error margin
Battery Life
Minimum 24 hours operation
8. Data Reliability and Safety
Discuss:
 Sensor calibration
 Data reliability
 Privacy considerations
9. Conclusion
Summarize:
 Importance of standardization
 Benefits for consumers and manufact

urers
10.Submission Instructions :
Participants must submit their report in PDF format.
Submission will be done through the Google Form shared via email. 
Submission Link:
https://forms.gle/Us5zR6FFGeNevcSs6  
The submission deadline is 12 March 2026.  
Important     Rules      
 The report will be checked for plagiarism and AI-generated content.

 The similarity score must be below 20%.
 Submissions exceeding the limit may be disqualified.
Evaluation     Criteria      
Submissions will be evaluated based on:
 Understanding of the topic
 Quality of proposed standards\n\n Technical clarity
 Practical testing methods
 Structure and presentation
Prizes
1st Prize - 2,000₹
2nd Prize - 1,50

0₹
3rd Prize - 1,000₹
Certificates will be provided to all participants!
Reference     Material      
Participants should refer to the attached Pre-Standardisation Study case studies shared in the 
drive link with this document. These references provide an idea of:
 Structure of a standard study
 Level of technical detail expected
 How standards

are proposed
Drive Link:
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1GPTRElPtDFSpJA0gA4nU3i4jFcRg_7J8?usp=drive_link  \n\n
